titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 754

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=996 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.397407+00 | translation moa=997 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.410549+00

p.cl:
Henricus comes filius regis scotie . episcopis . abbatibus . prioribus . comitibus . baronibus .
iusticiis . vicecomitibus . ministris . et omnibus probis hominibus tocius regni scotię
salutem .

Swæn presbiterum coram me et in mea presen
tia quietas clamasse monachis de coldingham et illis reddidisse
apud berewihc fiswihe cum omnibus ad illam pertinentibus in terris et
aquis et dimidiam prenderghest . et terram quam habuit in coldingham
et lummesdene

Volo itaque quatinus predicti Swen omni uexatione
remota terras suas prenominatas teneant et in pace possideant
Test canc' . Hingelramo . et Willelmo de sumeruilla .
Henry the earl, son of the king of Scotland, to bishops, abbots, priors, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, officials, and all worthy men of the entire realm of Scotland, greeting.
Be it know to you all that

Swain the priest, in front of me and in my presence, quitclaimed to the monks of Coldingham, and gave back to them at Berwick, Fishwick with all things pertaining to it in lands and waters; and half of Prenderguest; and the land which he had in Coldingham and Lumsdaine.
I therefore will that, with every vexatious action of the aforesaid Swain removed, they hold and possess their aforenamed lands in peace.
As witness the chancellor, Ingram, and William de Somerville.
